items.title: SpiceJet fined Rs 1 lakh by consumer watchdog CCPA for using 'dark patterns' in flight bookings
items.summary: The Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) has imposed a Rs 1 lakh penalty on SpiceJet for using "dark patterns" on its flight booking platform. The consumer watchdog found that the airline employed deceptive interface practices that could mislead users and influence their purchasing decisions, violating consumer protection norms.

items.summary: Backed by Emirates NBD, RBL Bank has raised $150 million through FCNR deposits, expanding its NRI funding base while focusing on trade finance, margin recovery and stabilising its card portfolio.

items.title: SBI Capital Markets, HDFC Bank among 4 book runners appointed for NLC India Renewables IPO
items.summary: NLC India Renewables has engaged four investment banks to facilitate its much-anticipated initial public offering. This IPO is designed to enhance the company's capital structure, fueling its ambitious growth objectives. The firm aims to broaden its renewable energy initiatives and align with national energy goals, currently managing 1,785 MW in solar, wind, and battery storage systems. Plans for aggressive expansion are in motion across various states in India.

items.title: Reliance Q1 results: Profit falls 22% YoY to ₹20,946 crore
items.summary: Reliance Q1 results: Consolidated profit declined 22.40% YoY to ₹20,946 crore. Sequentially, RIL's Q1FY27 profit rose by 23.42%. RIL's revenue from operations for the June quarter of FY27 rose by 25.41% YoY and 4.43% QoQ to ₹3,11,850 crore.

items.title: India's gold jewellery demand revives as prices stabilise; festive orders pick up: WGC
items.summary: India's gold jewellery demand is showing signs of recovery as stable prices and retailer discounts draw consumers back to stores, the World Gold Council said. Festive season orders have begun picking up, while gold ETF inflows and digital gold purchases also rebounded despite weaker demand for bars and coins.

items.title: RIL Q1 Results: Profit falls 22% YoY to Rs 20,946 crore due to one-time exceptional item; revenue up 25%
items.summary: RIL Q1 Results: Mukesh Ambani-led Reliance Industries Ltd (RIL) on Friday reported a 22% year-on-year (YoY) decline in its consolidated net profit at Rs 20,946 crore for the first quarter.

items.title: J&K Bank sells 0.5% stake in PNB MetLife for Rs 120 crore
items.summary: Jammu and Kashmir Bank has sold a 0.5% stake in PNB MetLife India Insurance to MetLife International Holdings for Rs 120 crore. The transaction reduces the bank’s holding to 2.5% without changing control of the life insurance company.

items.title: US stocks today: US stocks open lower as chip rout extends
items.summary: Wall Street opened lower as investors reassessed the strength of the AI-driven rally, triggering a fresh selloff in chip stocks. Nvidia and Intel declined, while Netflix dropped after a weak forecast. The Nasdaq led losses, reflecting pressure on tech stocks amid rising uncertainty and cautious market sentiment.

items.title: Hydrogen train: Railways to soon announce date for regular service
items.summary: Northern Railway will soon announce the start of commercial operations for India's first hydrogen train. Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the train on the Jind-Sonipat route in Haryana. The hydrogen train will depart from Jind at 7:40 am and arrive at Sonipat at 9:40 am. Its fare is expected to match existing local train fares, ranging from Rs 10 to Rs 35.

items.summary: Wall Street banks are on track for record trading revenue, driven by AI investing, dealmaking and active markets rather than crisis conditions. While profits remain strong, analysts caution that crowded AI trades, rising volatility and inflation risks could challenge markets later in 2026.

items.summary: Caliber Mining and Logistics' IPO showed strong initial demand, with bids for 96.54 lakh shares against 74.29 lakh shares offered. The IPO, set at ₹402-424, will close on July 21. Grey market premium indicates a potential listing price of ₹529, 38% above the issue price.

items.title: MakeMyTrip India files confidential IPO DRHP with Sebi. Check details
items.summary: MakeMyTrip has stealthily submitted documents for its Indian subsidiary's IPO, marking a significant step in expanding its market presence. The planned offering includes equity shares in MakeMyTrip India Ltd. By doing so, the company aims to boost brand recognition and motivate talent within the subsidiary. The funds raised will bolster MakeMyTrip's financial strength, paving the way for future development opportunities.

items.title: Sebi warns listed firms, regulated entities against rising 'Boss Scam' cyber fraud
items.summary: Sebi has issued a warning to all regulated entities regarding a rising cyber fraud known as the 'Boss Scam'. In this scheme, fraudsters masquerade as senior executives to deceive finance teams into executing fund transfers. The scam employs various channels such as email, WhatsApp, and even sophisticated AI tools like voice cloning to execute their tricks.

items.title: Quote of the day by Joel Tillinghast: "People who have had success in other parts of their lives have difficulty accepting how much failure there is in the stock market"
items.summary: Joel Tillinghast notes successful individuals struggle with stock market losses. The stock market involves uncertainty, economic cycles, and investor sentiment. Even experienced investors face setbacks and must learn from mistakes. Retail investors should embrace patience and diversification for wealth building.

items.title: Tata Technologies Q1 Results: Profit rises 6% to Rs 180 crore
items.summary: Tata Technologies has achieved an impressive 6.2 percent increase in profits for the June quarter, alongside a notable rise in consolidated revenues year-over-year. A strategic partnership worth USD 100 million with Tenneco showcases the company's dynamic approach. Furthermore, a leading Japanese automotive OEM has chosen Tata Technologies for a comprehensive vehicle engineering program, paving the way for robust future revenue growth.

items.title: India bonds sink on oil spike, index-entry uncertainty
items.summary: On Friday, Indian government bonds underwent a notable drop, with benchmark yields marking the steepest weekly rise in the past eight weeks. The surge in oil prices and concerns over index inclusion led traders to exercise caution, reducing their risk exposure as they approached the weekend. This trend is indicative of wider economic challenges affecting global markets.

items.title: WeWork India posts Rs 4.3 cr loss in Jun quarter
items.summary: In its latest quarterly report, WeWork India has revealed a net loss of Rs 4.30 crore, which is a notable reduction compared to last year's loss of Rs 14.10 crore. Total income for the April-June period rose to Rs 700.74 crore, while operational expenses increased to Rs 704.77 crore, illustrating the company's ongoing adaptations within the coworking sector.

items.title: Oberoi Realty Q1 profit rises 29% YoY to Rs 544 crore
items.summary: Oberoi Realty has reported an impressive 29% increase in its consolidated net profit for the June quarter, reaching Rs 543.51 crore, marking a notable improvement from last year. The company's total income soared to Rs 1,361.69 crore in the first quarter of the fiscal year, illustrating a robust financial performance. Renowned as one of India's leading real estate developers, Oberoi Realty continues to thrive in a competitive market.

items.title: Absence of train ticket alone cannot defeat railway-accident claim: SC
items.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a missing train ticket does not bar compensation for accident victims. This decision strengthens passenger rights and awards compensation to a widow. The court emphasized a liberal interpretation of the Railways Act as beneficial legislation. It also highlighted the railways' duty and passenger responsibility for safety.

items.title: EPFO launches Vishwas 2026; scheme to be operational for six months
items.summary: Under VISHWAS, 2026, damages or penalty for defaults pertaining to the period prior to June 14 2024 shall be recalculated at substantially reduced rates, namely 0.25% per month for defaults up to two months, 0.50% per month for defaults from two to less than four months, and 1.00% per month for defaults exceeding four months, the ministry of of labour and employment said on Friday.

items.summary: Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Tamil Nadu lead NITI Aayog's Investment Friendliness Index. Gujarat secured the top position due to its strong infrastructure and financial health. NITI Aayog released the report on Friday, highlighting key state performances. The investment rate in India currently stands at approximately twenty-five percent. Increased investments are crucial for boosting overall economic demand in the country.

Several states signed MoUs, including Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh. A textile recycling firm announced a substantial investment of Rs 4,800 crore.

items.title: Power producer Resolven bags 200 MW wind project from SECI
items.summary: Independent power producer Resolven has secured a 200 megawatt wind project from state-owned SECI. This Inter-State Transmission System connected capacity was bagged at a competitive tariff of Rs 3.85 per kWh. The project will be executed under a 25-year Power Purchase Agreement and is scheduled for commissioning by June 2028. Resolven's under-construction pipeline now stands at 1.8 gigawatts after this latest addition.

items.title: U.S.-China AI feud sees ASML walk tightrope between sales and geopolitics
items.summary: ASML is set to make around a fifth of its 2026 net sales from China, but geopolitical headwinds present a delicate situation.

items.title: India's forex reserves rise $964 million to $675.16 billion for week ended July 10
items.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves increased by $964 million to $675.16 billion. Foreign currency assets, the largest component, grew by $930 million during the week. Gold reserves also recorded an increase, rising by $24 million to $105.23 billion. Special Drawing Rights and reserve tranche position with the IMF saw minor increases. Earlier this year, reserves had touched a record high of $728.494 billion.

items.title: West Asia crisis, uncertain monsoon major risks for growth: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: Reserve Bank Governor Sanjay Malhotra noted West Asia crisis and weak monsoon pose growth risks. India's economy grew over seven percent recently, supported by strong fundamentals. The central bank projects 6.6 percent GDP growth for the current financial year. Inflation forecasts are raised to 5.1 percent, driven by supply-side factors. The rupee remains stable globally, and FDI inflows are robust.

items.title: A $5 trillion idea to save energy is hiding in plain sight
items.summary: Energy efficiency offers a simple, effective solution to rising global energy demands. This approach can create trillions in economic value and millions of jobs. It also significantly reduces climate pollution and premature deaths from air quality. Government policies and programs are essential for achieving these substantial energy savings.

items.title: Federal Bank among 4 stocks that hit 52-week highs and rallied up to 25% in a month
items.summary: Sensex ended 96 points higher on Friday as four BSE 200 stocks—Oracle Financial Services, Adani Energy Solutions, BHEL and Federal Bank—hit fresh 52-week highs. The milestone reflects sustained buying interest, improving investor sentiment and continued bullish momentum in these counters.

items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday said India's railway network would have faced a major crisis during the recent West Asia conflict if it had remained dependent on diesel, highlighting that nearly 99% of Indian Railways has been electrified over the past 12 years.

items.title: Crisil Ratings upgrades long-term ratings of Vedanta group companies
items.summary: Crisil Ratings has upgraded Vedanta group companies' long-term ratings to AA+ and assigned a stable outlook. This upgrade reflects the strong financial profile and improved financial flexibility of the demerged entities. Vedanta Ltd anchors the group with market leadership and a significant stake in Hindustan Zinc. Leverage is expected to remain comfortably below 1.0x over the medium term. The group's diversified metals portfolio strengthens its overall operating profile.

items.summary: Armed assailants boarded the chemical tanker Asana in the Gulf of Aden off southern Yemen on Friday and are believed to have taken control of the vessel, according to maritime security sources.

items.summary: The Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) has launched 'VISHWAS, 2026', a one-time dispute resolution scheme aimed at encouraging employers to settle long-pending damages and penalty-related cases through a simplified digital process, while promoting voluntary compliance and reducing litigation.

items.title: Oberoi Realty Q1 profit jumps 29% to ₹544 crore, revenue rises 32%; declares ₹2 interim dividend
items.summary: Oberoi Realty reported Q1 FY27 net profit of ₹544 crore, up 29% YoY. Revenue rose 31.7% to ₹1,301 crore, with EBITDA increasing 41% to ₹734 crore. The company also declared an interim dividend of ₹2 per share as it continues to see strong demand for luxury homes.

items.title: CAFE III norms signal faster EV shift in India while balancing automakers concerns: Nomura
items.summary: CAFE refers to government regulations that require automakers to meet a specific average level of fuel economy or CO₂ emissions across their entire fleet of vehicles sold in a year. Under the draft notification, compliance will be evaluated over FY28-30 and FY30-32, giving manufacturers additional time to phase in electric vehicle (EV) launches.

items.title: JSW Steel Q1 result: Net Profit doubles to ₹4,696 crore; revenue jumps 10%
items.summary: JSW Steel reported a Q1 FY27 net profit of ₹4,696 crore, more than double last year’s ₹2,209 crore, driven by a 9.8% revenue increase to ₹47,364 crore. The company began construction on a new 2-million-tonne steel plant in Andhra Pradesh as part of its expansion.

items.title: Rupee closes at 96.28 vs USD; logs sharpest weekly drop since May as oil price jump stings
items.summary: The Indian rupee faced its most significant weekly drop since May, driven by rising oil prices and strong demand for the dollar. Increasing tensions between the U.S. and Iran exacerbated concerns over energy supplies, further straining the rupee's position. However, interventions from state-run banks provided some relief, temporarily halting its downward trend. Market participants are closely monitoring oil prices and the Reserve Bank of India’s approach to the rupee’s depreciation.

The move signals confidence despite the stock’s weak performance, earnings misses and challenges across retail and oil-to-chemicals businesses.

items.title: India's smartphone shipments fall 10% in Q2, biggest June-quarter drop in six years
items.summary: India's smartphone shipments saw a significant ten percent decline year-on-year. Rising memory prices forced brands to increase handset prices, impacting demand. The budget segment experienced a substantial forty-five percent drop in shipments. Samsung was the only major brand to achieve shipment growth during this period.

items.title: Hang Seng crashes over 2% as tech selloff hits Asia markets. Why Hong Kong stocks are falling on Friday?
items.summary: Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2% by midday on Friday, weighed down by a global selloff in technology stocks as renewed inflation concerns dampened risk appetite. The Hang Seng Tech Index plunged 4%, with investors reducing exposure to technology and other growth stocks amid fears that higher inflation could lead to tighter monetary policy and higher interest rates.

items.title: Global Market: South Korea's AI-driven stock frenzy fuels volatility, challenges market fundamentals
items.summary: The sharp swings have unsettled investors and regulators alike, with market movements increasingly driven by leveraged investment products rather than corporate fundamentals. The volatility has also complicated the assessment of South Korean equities, particularly as the country remains a critical supplier of AI-related semiconductor technology.

items.title: Global Market: Euro zone current account surplus widens in May; market impact likely limited
items.summary: The euro zone's current account surplus increased in May, driven by stronger primary income. This rise offset a narrower trade surplus, according to European Central Bank data. On an unadjusted basis, a deficit was recorded for the month. Over twelve months, the surplus represented 1.7% of gross domestic product. Stock markets are not expected to react significantly to this data.

items.title: India-UK CETA takes effect: First zero-duty Indian coffee, jewellery consignments reach UK shores
items.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement has officially come into force. Zero-duty consignments of Indian jewellery and coffee have now landed in the United Kingdom. This landmark pact is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade between the two nations. Businesses anticipate substantial growth and increased market competitiveness following the agreement. The pact aims to strengthen supply chains and support job creation across both countries.

items.summary: By the close of 2026, India and the European Union will launch a transformative free trade agreement, taking effect in early 2027. This pact is designed to create vast business prospects, with 93% of Indian exports obtaining duty-free entry into the EU market. Luxury items such as cars and wines will become more affordable for consumers in India.

The Vande Mataram national song will receive statutory protection similar to the national anthem. New legislation aims to tighten control over foreign contributions to non-governmental organizations. Stricter regulations for delayed birth and death registrations are also planned. A major overhaul of higher education will dissolve existing regulatory bodies into one commission.

The value for March 2026 reached 70.0, up from 67.0 in March 2025. Growth was observed across all sub-indices, reflecting deepening financial inclusion. Usage of financial services significantly contributed to this overall improvement.

Vietnamese rice prices are unchanged as buyers await increased harvest supplies.
